logs.name: greasy-clapper
logs.text:
But if no one knows how a QB will perform than why is it so important that they were willing to pay a premium for him when he ultimately didn't play especially well for them anyway?

Teams make stupid signings all the time.  I'm a Mets fan and they're still paying a premium for Bobby Bonilla.  Hardly a great move.  Sure, the Chiefs played

logs.name: concave-log
logs.text:
Your view seems to be completely unfalsifiable. Yes, Brady has been fortunate to have a great team and organization around him - he's not the only QB to spend his career in an advantageous situation, but his is certainly one of if not the best.

So what? The only argument you've made is that the Patriots have done well during limited stretches with lesser QBs. Well, okay. Brady has no control over that, so it seems absurd to judge him by it. Like, would the Patriots have done poorly if Peyton Manning was their QB in the same circumstances? Would that somehow make Manning better? It doesn't make any sense.

Of course an otherwise excellent team can still have some success with a subpar QB. That proves the Patriots have been excellent, which nobody ever disputed.

It doesn't prove anything about Brady. As you seem to acknowledge, there's no significant measure, in terms of success, accolades, or statistics, by which Brady does not rank among the very best of all-time. There are literally *six* other QBs who have won at least *half* as many games as him.

What could he possibly have done better to convince you he's one of the best?

logs.name: chilly-shotgun
logs.text: "Are you kidding me? You think you're some kind of expert just because you can spew out stats and accolades? Newsflash, concave-log: just because someone has a good team around them doesn't mean they're the one doing the heavy lifting. And by the way, Brady's had some pretty questionable decisions on the field too, but you're too busy trying to justify his ego to notice. And yeah, I'm not disputing the Patriots' success, but that doesn't make Brady a great QB. He's a guy who's been lucky to have a system that's worked for him, and he's not even the most consistent QB in the league. So, no, I don't think he's one of the best of all-time, and I'm not convinced by your weak arguments."

logs.name: greasy-clapper
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, chilly-shotgun? You think you can just come in here and trash Brady like that? I've spent decades studying his career, and I know what I'm talking about. You're just some armchair quarterback who thinks he can just dismiss all the accolades and stats without putting in the work. Newsflash: Brady's a winner, and winners don't get criticized like this. You want to talk about questionable decisions? How about Tom's decision to leave the Patriots after 20 years? That was a bold move, and it's not something just anyone can do. So, no, I won't be swayed by your weak opinions. Brady's a legend, and if you can't see that, then maybe you should just stick to watching the game instead of trying to be a critic."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:
""
```
@greasy-clapper, please refrain from personal attacks and keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand. @chilly-shotgun, your points are valid, but let's ensure we maintain a respectful dialogue. Let's focus on the performance and contributions of the players rather than individual egos.
```

logs.name: sunny-causeway
logs.text: "@greasy-clapper, I'm not trying to trash Brady, I'm just stating my opinion. I think his recent performance has been overhyped. I've seen better quarterback play from guys like Rodgers and Brees. And by the way, Brady's decision to leave the Patriots was a huge mistake, it's what led to his decline in my opinion. You can't just dismiss my opinion because you're a 'decades-long' Patriots fanboy. I'm not trying to be a critic, I'm just sharing my thoughts. Can we please keep the discussion civil?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:
""

User @sunny-causeway, thank you for clarifying your stance. Let's aim to keep the conversation respectful and focused on the performance and contributions of the players. @greasy-clapper, while it's important to have strong opinions, let's avoid personal attacks and focus on the facts and performances discussed. We can all learn from each other's perspectives when we engage constructively.

logs.name: chilly-shotgun
logs.text: "@sunny-causeway, I'm not a Patriots fanboy, I just know what I've seen. Brady's decision to leave was a huge mistake, plain and simple. You can't just dismiss his entire career because you think he had a better QB in Rodgers or Brees. Those guys have had different teams and circumstances, it's not fair to compare them directly. And by the way, Brady's still one of the greatest QBs of all time, no matter what you think."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "User @chilly-shotgun, while it's understandable to stand by your beliefs, let's ensure our discussion remains respectful and fact-based. Comparing players across different eras and team dynamics can be subjective. Let's focus on discussing their individual performances and contributions without dismissing each other's viewpoints."

logs.name: sunny-causeway
logs.text: "@chilly-shotgun, I'm not dismissing your opinion, I'm just saying that Brady's decision to leave was a huge mistake because he's still playing at an elite level and it's not like he's getting older or anything. You can't just cherry-pick stats and ignore the fact that he's still the G.O.A.T. in the eyes of many fans. And by the way, Rodgers and Brees are great QBs, but they don't have the rings to match Brady's 7. Let's not forget that."

logs.name: tense-bounce
logs.text: "@sunny-causeway, I'm not cherry-picking stats, I'm stating a fact - Brady's decision to leave the Pats was a huge mistake, and his age is definitely a factor. You can't just ignore the fact that he's not playing at the same level as he was in his prime. And by the way, Rodgers and Brees may have rings, but they don't have the same level of dominance as Brady. You're just drinking the Patriots Kool-Aid, my friend."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:
""

"@tense-bounce, please refrain from personal attacks and maintain a respectful tone in your posts. Let's keep the discussion focused on the topic at hand. @sunny-causeway and @chilly-shotgun, thank you for your contributions. Please continue to engage in a constructive manner."

logs.name: tense-sump
logs.text: "@moderator, chill out, dude. I was just stating the obvious. You're too busy being the'moderator police' to let people have a real conversation. @sunny-causeway, Brady's decline is a fact, but so is the fact that you're a Patriots fanboy who can't handle the truth. Rodgers and Brees are legends, but they're not Tom Brady. Get over it."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "User tense-sump, please refrain from making personal attacks and maintaining a respectful tone in your posts. We aim to keep discussions constructive and focused on the topic. @sunny-causeway, thank you for your contribution."
